name: Chocolate Kahlua Cheesecake
recipeInstructions:
1. Mix crumbs, butter, and sugar. Press onto bottom and up the sides partway of a 9-inch springform pan.
2. Freeze for 5 minutes, then bake at 350°F for ten minutes and allow to cool.
3. Heat in a bowl or pan over boiling water 3/4 cup chocolate chunks until melted and smooth. Add 1/4 cup whipping cream and 1/4 cup Kahlua and stir until blended.
4. In another bowl, beat cream cheese and sugar until fluffy. Add first mixture to the second and blend well.
5. Add eggs one at a time, mixing well after each addition, and vanilla. Pour over prepared crust and bake for 10 minutes at 400°F.
6. Decrease temperature to 275°F and bake another 45 minutes. Remove from oven to cool; loosen cake from rim of pan and remove.
7. Melt remaining chocolate. Then add 1/4 cup whipping cream and the 2 tablespoons Kahlua, mixing well.
8. Spread on top of cake and refrigerate for six or so hours.
9. Before serving, beat remaining whipping cream with the powdered sugar and garnish cake by piping it on (personally, I'll probably skip this step) and decorating with a few remaining chocolate chips. Refrigerate leftovers.
